info.title: RcodeZero DNS - REST API
info.description:
This is Version 2 of the Rcode0 API. Version 1 (except the ACME calls) is deprecated and will not receive new features anymore Get the latest specification file [here](/openapi/rcode0api-latest.json) Please also read our workflow [documentation](https://www.rcodezero.at/en/support/documentation/workflow) and our [FAQs](https://www.rcodezero.at/en/support/faq) ### Changelog | Version | Date | Author | Changes | | --------- | :------------- | :------: | -------------- | | 2.0 | 2020-05-13 | Michael Braunoeder | Initial Release v2.

Changes:
  • `POST /api/v2/zones`: Return assigned NSSet in response
  • New call "disable zone" (and return zone state on "get zone details" call)
  • New query parameter "ignoresafetyperiod" for "sign zone" call to get DNSKEY and DS record immediately in the response
  • New query parameter "dnssec" for "add zone" call to sign a newly added zone immediately and return the DNSKEY and DS record in the response
| | 2.1 | 2020-06-08 | Michael Braunoeder | Changes:
  • New call: `/api/v2/zones/{zone}/status` - check if a zone is disabled or not
  • New call: `/api/v2/reports/domainlist` - get an unpaginated list of configured domains
| | 2.2 | 2021-09-13 | Michael Braunoeder | Changes:
  • Added missing attribute 'created' to call: `GET /api/v2/zones/{zone}`
| | 2.3 | 2021-09-14 | Michael Braunoeder | Changes:
  • `GET /api/v2/zones`:
    • new parameters - filter[...] and orderby[...]
    • Response returns problematic_domains flag
  • /api/v2/reports/domainlist: return serial in response
  • Return status of KSK in zone details
| | 2.4 | 2022-11-02 | Michael Braunoeder | Changes:
  • `GET /api/v2/zones/`: new parameters - orderby[serial]
| | 2.5 | 2023-05-02 | Michael Braunoeder | Changes:
  • add support for CDS/CDNSKEY
  • add `serial_auto_increment` option to zones
  • add support for incoming TSIG-keys (+cleanup TSIG-interface)
  • New Call: `PUT /api/v2/zones/{zone}/rrsets`: Replace RRSets for zone
  • `/api/v2/zones/{zone}/unsign`: add `force` parameter
  • Limit `PATCH /api/v2/zones/{zone}/rrsets` to max 1000 rrsets per request
  • New call: `GET messages/list`: get a list of messages in the message queue
Deprecated
  • `/api/v2/zones/{zone}/stats/magnitude`
  • `/api/v2/zones/{zone}/stats/qnames`
  • `/api/v2/zones/{zone}/stats/nxdomains`
  • `/api/v2/stats/topqnames`
  • `/api/v2/stats/topnxdomains`
  • `/api/v2/stats/topmagnitude`
  • `/api/v2/tsig/out`
  • `/api/v2/tsig/out/{keyname}`
  • `/api/v2/settings/tsigout`
| | 2.6 | 2023-08-31 | Michael Braunoeder | Changes:
  • add `dnssec_zsk` to `GET /api/v2/zones/{zone}` response
| | 2.7 | 2023-11-15 | Michael Braunoeder | Changes:
  • `GET /api/v2/zones`: `filter[domain]` add special characters `^` and `$` to support "domain starts/end with"-searches
| | 2.8 | 2025-02-12 | Michael Braunoeder | Changes:
  • `GET /api/v2/stats/topzones`: remove `id` from response
| | 2.9 | 2025-02-19 | Michael Braunoeder | Changes:
  • `GET api/v2/reports/nxdomains` , `GET api/v2/reports/queryrates`, `GET /api/v2/reports/accounting`: add parameter `type=json` to get results in JSON format
  • `GET api/v2/reports/queryrates`: add paramter `include_nx=1`to number of NX queries too
|

get.description:
returns a list of configured zones

Note: This call is paginated

parameters.name: page_size
parameters.description: change the number of items per page. Default 100 (-1 disables paging)

parameters.name: filter[domain]
parameters.description:
filter results based on substring search on domainname.
`^` matches the begin of the domainname,
`$`matches the end of the domainname
(e.g. `.at$`: get a list of domains with the TLD .at)

parameters.name: filter[dnssec]
parameters.description:
filter results based on DNSSEC state.
`yes` returns only zones using RcodeZero's DNSSEC signing service.
`presigned` returns only zones which are already signed by the customer
`no` returns only zones that are un-signed.
Not using this filter will return signed and unsigned zones

parameters.name: filter[zone_disabled]
parameters.description: filter results based on zone_disabled flag

parameters.name: filter[problematic_domain]
parameters.description: filter results on problematic domain flag

parameters.name: orderby[next_check]
parameters.description: order list by next schedules check timestamp

parameters.name: orderby[nsset]
parameters.description: order list by assigned rcode0 nsset

post.description:
Adds a new zone (master or slave) to the anycast network.

When a master zone is added, a zone skeleton (SOA and NS record sets) is created automatically.

When the zone which should be added is already provisioned on RcodeZero (under management by another RcodeZero account), the system will by default rejects the request with a respective error message. To allow the transfer (change of ownership) from one RcodeZero account to another, a transfer code has to be added to the zone (by the zone owner, thus the code should be added before transferring a domain to a new account). The transfer code has to be added as TXT record for the subdomain “rcode0” and has to look like this:

```
rcode0 IN TXT "allowed=XXXXXXXXX" 
```

where XXXXXXXXX is the md5-hash of the RcodeZero username of the new account. 
If the transfer code check was successful, the domain will be switched from one customer to the other, the master server IP(s) will be changed to the new ones, and a zone transfer will be performed.

Transfer code example: In this example the domain to be transferred is “example.com” and the username of the gaining RcodeZero customer is “best-isp.com”:

```
rcode0.example.com. IN TXT "allowed=15c0bd9371ff658a5634ae16e7632ca6"
```

parameters.name: dnssec
parameters.description: When set to 1, the created zone will be signed immediately and there is no need to consider any TTLs. Hence, the DNSKEY and DS record will be immediately released and returned in the response. There will not be a DSUPDATE notification for this zone, but you still have to call zones/{zone}/dsupdate after you have added the DS record to the parent zone.

parameters.name: test
parameters.description: when set to 1 the given zone is validated against the Rcode0 policy if it could be added or not (e.g. because there is already a parent zone, zone already configured, ...). The given zone is NOT added to the Rcode0 network.

get.summary: Get details of a configured zone

example.dnssec_status_detail: Signed with NSEC3, no opt-out (RSASHA256 with 2048 bits KSK and 1024 bits ZSK)
example.dnssec_ksk_status_detail: The new keys are safe to be published in the parent zone. Please submit the DNSKEY/DS key to the parent zone and acknowledge the submission by pressing the 'Acknowledge' button.

put.description:
To change a zone from type=master to type=slave provide

```
{
    "type": "slave",
    "masters": ["193.0.2.2","2001:db8::2"]
}
```

to change the master ips if a zone is type=slave

```
{
    "masters": ["193.0.2.2","2001:db8::2"]
}

```
If a secondary zone has an incoming TSIG key configured, this configuration is deleted when converting the zone to a primary zone.

patch.description: Sets the zone status (enabled/disabled). An enabled zone is configured on the RcodeZero network and visible in the DNS. A disabled zone remains configured on the RcodeZero network but will not be visible in the DNS.

delete.description: removes a zone from the RcodeZero Anycast network

get.description:
get the RRsets for given zone. Works for master and slave zones.

Note: This call is paginated

parameters.name: names
parameters.description: return only rrset with given name(s)

parameters.name: types
parameters.description: return only RRsets with given type(s)

parameters.name: page_size
parameters.description: change the number of items per page. Default 100

patch.description:
adds/updates or deletes RRsets.

Only complete RRsets can be manipulated. So if a RRset consists of two A records and one A record is to be updated the update request must contain both A records.

When updating the SOA only the MName and Email values of the SOA record content and the TTL of the SOA record itself can be updated, updates for the other timer values will be ignored. Disabling a SOA record will also be ignored.

The RRSet must comply to the common RFCs and fulfiil checks like

  • check for trailing dots in the content
  • check format of MX and DNSKEY records
  • check for CNAME or another records for $label if a CNAME for $label is added
  • check for ALIAS for $label if ALIAS for $label is added
  • check MX / Null-MX if MX / Null-MX is added

Examples:

  • add a new RRset: [ { "name": "www.testzone1.at.", "type": "A", "ttl" : 3600, "changetype": "add", "records": [ { "content": "10.10.0.1" } ] } ]
  • update a RRSet: [ { "name": "www.testzone1.at.", "type": "A", "ttl" : 3600, "changetype": "update", "records": [ { "content": "10.10.0.10" }, { "content": "10.10.0.20" } ] } ]
  • delete a RRset: [ { "name": "www.testzone1.at.", "type": "A", "changetype": "delete" } ]
  • update a RRSet and disable one record: [ { "name": "www.testzone1.at.", "type": "A", "changetype": "update", "records": [ { "content": "10.10.0.10" }, { "content": "10.10.0.20", "disabled": true } ] } ]
A maximum of 1000 RRSets per requests are allowed.
A changelog with the last 100 RRset-Updates is available in the webinterface.

Requests to the same zone are serialized in the API to prevent race conditions. Hence, there is no great benefit if you send concurrent requests for the same zone. Further, if there are too many concurrent requests and serialization leads to more than 5s delay, the request is rejected.

put.description:
replace all RRsets in zone.

If `serial_auto_increment` for zone is disabled, the serial of the given SOA-RR will be set, otherwise the existing serial will be incremented. Attention: decreasing the serial will avoid customer secondaries updating the zones. A maximum of 3000 rrset could be added to the zone.
Note: `replace RRsets` does not write a changelog, it behaves like a zone transfer

delete.description: removes all RRsets (except SOA and NS) from the zone

example.message: all RRsets (expect SOA and NS) removed

post.description:
This endpoint works only with secondary zones and queues a zone transfer request for the given zone. The zone transfer will be performed usually within a few seconds, and will be tried only once by randomly choosing one of the configured primary name servers. If that single attempt fails, no further attemps will be performed.
The zone will be transfered to our inbound name server regardless of its serial, but the internal zone distribution is only reliable if the zone's serial on the customer's primary name server is higher than on the RcodeZero name server (for zones using the RcodeZero DNSSEC service it only needs to be higher than RcodeZero's 'serialincoming' serial, not the public facing serial).

NOTE: As said above, this endpoint is not a reliable way to decrease a zone's serial. To guarantee consistent zones over all RcodeZero location, the zone's serial MUST always be increased on zone changes.

get.summary: Get outbound zone transfer configuration for a zone
get.description: Show the configured outbound XFR settings for a zone

post.summary: Set outbound zone transfer configuration for a zone
post.description: Outbound Zone transfers can be configured on account or zone level. A specific per zone configuration overrules the account wide configuration (both secondary IPs and TSIG key). The name of the TSIG key must reference to a configured [outgoing TSIG key](##api-tsig-keys)replaces the outbound zone transfer configuration with the provided one. The request must contain a full configuration,  emtpy fields will remove possibly configured values (and the zone configuration will fall back to the default account wide configuration)

example.message: Outbound transfer settings have been updated

delete.description: Delete zone specific oubound zone transfers settings

example.message: Outbound transfer settings have been deleted

get.summary: Get inbound (into Rcode0) zone transfer configuration for a zone
get.description: Show the configured inboud XFR settings for a zone

post.summary: Set inbound zone transfer configuration for a zone
post.description: Inbound Zone transfers can be configured on account or zone level. A specific per zone configuration overrules the account wide configuration. The name of the TSIG key must reference to a configured [outgoing TSIG key](##api-tsig-keys)replaces the outbound zone transfer configuration with the provided one.

example.message: Inbound transfer settings have been updated

delete.description: Delete zone specific inbound zone transfers settings

example.message: Inbound transfer settings have been deleted

get.summary: check if a zone is disabled or not

post.description: Starts DNSSEC signing of a zone.

parameters.name: ignoresafetyperiod
parameters.description: When set to 1 the given zone will be signed immediately, ignoring the zone TTLs which are usually used to ensure resolution issues. Hence, the DNSKEY and DS record will be immediately released and returned in the response. There will not be a DSUPDATE notification for this zone, but you still have to call zones/{zone}/dsupdate after you have added the DS record to the parent zone. NOTE: Ignoring the safety period for DS pulication can cause resolution issues with DNSSEC validating resolvers. Only use this feature for testing, or if you know what you do.

parameters.name: enable_cds_cdnskey
parameters.description: When set to 1 the publication of CDS/CDNSKEY records for the given zone is enabled.

post.summary: stop signing a zone with DNSSEC
post.description: Stops DNSSEC signing of a zone, reverting the zone to unsigned. A zone will become safe to unsign if: there are no pending KSK rollovers, and it can be ensured that the DS disappeared from the resolvers (the date when we last saw the DS + the TTL of the DS + 18hours safty interval for zone propagation delay).

parameters.name: force
parameters.description: When set to 1 force the unsigning of the given zone even if the DS-records are still active. Use with caution, this may lead to resolving issues with DNSSEC validating resolvers. Only use this feature for testing, or if you know what you do.

post.description: Starts a DNSSEC key signing key rollover
Acknowledges a DS update. This automatically also deletes all DSUPDATE notifications for this domain from the message queue.

available on test system only:

Simulates that the DS records of all KSKs of a certain domain were seen in the parent zone. This allows to test key rollovers even if the DS of the currently active KSK was not seen in the parent zone. A DSSEEN event will be pushed ot the message queue.

example.message: simulate ok: Simulated DSSSEN. Had to update 1 keys for zone 'testzone1.at'

post.description:
available only on test system:

simulates that the DS records of all KSKs of a certain domain were removed from the parent zone. This allows to subsequently "unsign" a domain.

A DSREMOVED event will be pushed to the message queue.

example.message: simulate ok: Simulated DSREMOVED. Had to update 1 keys for zone 'testzone1.at'

post.description: Adds a new TSIG key to RcodeZero. The name of the key must be unique in the RcodeZero system. If the name is already taken (by another RcodeZero account) an error is raised.

get.description: show details of a given key.

parameters.name: keyname
parameters.description: name of the tsig key to be shown

put.description: Updates the alghoritm and secret of a given key.

parameters.name: keyname
parameters.description: name of the tsig key to be updated

delete.description: Deletes the given TSIG key from the system. The key is also removed from the zone configurations (if still configured, these zones will fall back to the default key, if configured) or as the default key.

parameters.name: keyname
parameters.description: name of the tsig key to be deleted

post.description: Adds a new TSIG key to RcodeZero. The name of the key must be unique in the RcodeZero system. If the name is already taken (by another RcodeZero account) an error is raised. The `default_key` attribute defines if this key should be the default key for the account and used for all zones without a zone specific outbound XFR configuration. Only one default key could be configured, the replace the current default key, delete the old one and add the new one.

put.description: Updates the alghoritm and secret of a given key.

parameters.name: keyname
parameters.description: name of the tsig key to be deleted

delete.description: Deletes the given TSIG key from the system. The key is also removed from the zone configurations (if still configured) and these zones will fall back to the default key.

parameters.name: keyname
parameters.description: name of the tsig key to be deleted

get.description: Get the total number of queries and the number of queries answered with NXDOMAIN for the given zone for the last 180 days (max.)
This call is deprecated and does not return any data
This call is deprecated and does not return any data
This call is deprecated and does not return any data

Return the Top 1000 zones from your account with the highest number of queries in the given past period.

Returns an empty array if no data is available

parameters.name: days
parameters.description: get statistics for the last X days (X between 1 and 180, default 30)

get.description: Get the total number of queries and the number of queries answered with NXDOMAIN for all zones in your account for the given past period.

parameters.name: days
parameters.description: get statistics for the last X days; (X between 1 and 180, default 30)

get.description:
Return the number of Queries grouped by the originating country/subregion and region for the given past period.

Returns an empty array if no data is available.

parameters.name: days
parameters.description: get statistics for the last X days;  (X between 1 and 180, default 30)

get.description: This call is deprecated and does not return any data

parameters.name: days
parameters.description: get statistics for the last X days; Valid values for X are 30, 60 and 180. Default 30

parameters.name: days
parameters.description: get statistics for the last X days; Valid values for X are 30, 60 and 180. Default 30

parameters.name: days
parameters.description: get statistics for the last X days; Valid values for X are 30, 60 and 180. Default 30

get.description: retrieves the oldest unacknowledged message from the message queue.

example.comment: Simulate that the DS record has been seen in the parent zone.

get.description:
returns a list of all messages in messagequeue

Note: This call is paginated

parameters.name: page_size
parameters.description: change the number of items per page. Default 100 (-1 disables paging)

data.comment: Insert or replace DS record in the parent zone.
DS record has been removed from the parent zone.
DS record has been removed from the parent zone.

delete.description: Acknowlegdes (and deletes) the message with the given id

get.description: Get global account settings

put.description: configures the account setting "secondaries". Those secondaries will receive notifies and may transfer out all zones under the management of the account.

put.summary: Set global TSIG key used for inbound transfers
put.description:
configures the TSIG key used for inbound zone transfers.

The TSIG key must be [added first](#post-/api/v2/tsig)

delete.summary: Removes global TSIG key used for inbound transfers
delete.description: removes the configured TSIG key use for inbound transfer. The TSIG key itself is configured, but it will not be used as global inbound key anymore

put.summary: Set global TSIG key used for outbound transfers
put.description:
configures the TSIG key used for outbound zone transfers.

The TSIG key must be [added first](#post-/api/v2/tsig)

delete.summary: Removes global TSIG key used for outbound transfers
delete.description: removes the configured TSIG key use for outbound transfer. The TSIG key itself is configured, but it will not be used as global inbound key anymore

put.description:
configures the TSIG key used for outbound zone transfers.

The value must be a comma separated string of key parameters with: “keyname,algorithm,secret_base64”.

get.description:
returns the list of problematic zones (=zones with could not be checked or transferred successfully from the master server)

Note: This call is paginated

parameters.name: page_size
parameters.description: change the number of items per page. Default 100

get.summary: NXDOMAIN queries for today or yesterday
get.description:
get all QNAMEs and QTYPE for your account which have been answered with NXDOMAIN for yesterday or today as CSV or JSON.

The report is updated once every hour.

parameters.name: day
parameters.description: get NXDOMAINs for today or yesterday; Valid values are 'today' or 'yesterday'. Defaults to 'today'

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: output format, either `csv` or `json` . Defaults to 'csv'

get.description:
get the accounting report per day for the given month as CSV or JSON

Parameter: month Values: 'YYYY-MM'

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: output format, either `csv` or `json` . Defaults to 'csv'

get.description:
get the number of queries per domain and day for the given month or day  as CSV or JSON.

Note: either month or day must be provided. Month must be provided in format YYYY-MM, day must be provided in the format YYYY-MM-DD. For example: month=2022-12, or day=2022-12-31.

parameters.name: include_nx
parameters.description: set to 1 to add the number of NX queries to the report. Defaults to '0'

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: output format, either `csv` or `json` . Defaults to 'csv'

get.description: get an unpaginated list of configured domains

dnssec.title: dnssec
dnssec.description: is the zone signed by the RcodeZero Anycast Network

dnssec_safe_to_unsign.title: dnssec_safe_to_unsign
dnssec_safe_to_unsign.description: To avoid outages caused by a premature DNSSEC removal, RcodeZero rejects unsign operation until the caches are expired. This flag indicates if an unsign action will be allowed at the current time. A zone will become safe to unsign if: there are no pending KSK rollovers, and it can be ensured that the DS disappeared from the resolvers (the date when we last saw the DS + the TTL of the DS + 18hours safty interval for zone propagation delay).

dnssec_status.title: dnssec_status
dnssec_status.description: whether or not zone is signed by RcodeZero Anycast

key.description: current Zone Signing Key used for signing the zone

keyid.description: Key ID of the used ZSK

active.description: If true, this key will be used for signing

tags.name: API: ACME
tags.description: this endpoints are special endpoints with limited permissions used for dns01-challenge for Let’s Encrypt and the ACME protocol. These endpoints can only be accessed by API tokens with the `ACME`permission and are used by the variuos RcodeZero backend implementaions for Let's Enrypt certificate issuers
